content: Never commit Python cache files (__pycache__) to version control — add __pycache__/ to .gitignore and remove these files from the repository to keep it clean
Always verify all imported modules and dependencies exist before referencing them — missing files like database.py, models.py, schemas.py cause critical import errors and application failures

Never change frameworks without implementing the complete backend infrastructure — switching from FastAPI to Flask requires implementing all routes, models, and app setup before templates can function
Always complete test files without truncation — incomplete files with truncated functions cause syntax errors and break test runs
Use proper foreign key relationships in tests instead of hardcoded IDs — hardcoded values like venue_id=1, concert_id=1 don't test actual relationships and will fail with real database constraints
Always implement CSRF protection on forms/HTMX requests and XSS escaping for user content — use Flask-WTF CSRF tokens and template escaping like {{ content|e }}

Mock chained method calls require explicit return_value assignment at each level — use mock_db.query.return_value.filter.return_value.first.return_value instead of mock_db.query().filter().first.return_value

Use FastAPI dependency injection for database sessions instead of manual session management — calling next(get_db()) and db.close() manually violates SQLAlchemy session patterns and should use dependency injection or context manager
Never use CORS allow_origins=['*'] in production code — either use specific origins or make CORS configuration environment-dependent for security
Test public interface methods rather than private methods — avoid testing private methods directly and instead test them through public methods that use them
Never remove essential framework dependencies without replacement — removing core dependencies like pytest, alembic, pydantic, httpx breaks testing and database functionality
Always implement pagination on collection GET endpoints — missing limit/offset parameters on endpoints like GET /concerts/ can cause performance issues with large datasets
Implement foundational layers before dependent components — create models, services, and backend endpoints before writing tests or frontend code that depends on them
Define shared Base classes in a central module like models/base.py rather than locally with declarative_base() — local definitions cause import issues when other models need the same Base
Use request body with proper schema instead of query parameters for POST endpoints — POST endpoint should use proper schema in request body rather than query parameters
Validate data integrity constraints at the model level — enforce business rules like preventing negative durations and implementing proper field validation
Use FastAPI lifespan context manager instead of deprecated @app.on_event('startup') for FastAPI 0.104.1+ — the on_event decorators are being phased out
Never mix framework-specific syntax between different frameworks — using Flask url_for() in FastAPI templates causes incompatibility errors
API endpoints should return JSON data following REST conventions — returning raw HTML from API endpoints violates standards and makes testing/maintenance difficult
Remove unused imports to keep code clean — avoid importing components like pydantic EmailStr and validator if not implementing validation
Quality gate failures occurred — e2e_tests: 0/6 passed, coverage: 0% (threshold: 70%)
Always ensure database migration enum values match model definitions exactly — migration used 'PLANNING' but model used 'planning', causing runtime failures
Use modern SQLAlchemy 2.0 DeclarativeBase instead of deprecated declarative_base() — import from sqlalchemy.orm and create class Base(DeclarativeBase)
Never submit PRs with failing tests or truncated files — complete all test implementations and ensure they pass before requesting review
Always complete test files without truncation — incomplete files with truncated functions cause syntax errors and break test runs
Make hardcoded business values configurable through constants or environment variables — avoid magic numbers like ticket prices and rates embedded in code
Make CORS configuration environment-dependent with configurable origins — avoid hardcoded localhost origins for security and deployment flexibility
Always verify all imported modules exist before committing code — importing from .api.concerts, .api.venues, ..database, and ..models when these files don't exist in the PR causes import failures
Always use proper HTML escaping when constructing HTML with user data — using f-strings without escaping for venue names and notes creates XSS vulnerabilities
Never commit Python cache files to version control — __pycache__/ directories should be added to .gitignore and removed from repository

Always verify all imported modules and dependencies exist in the PR before referencing them — importing models.py, database.py, and get_db() function that don't exist causes import errors and prevents application startup

Always include referenced template files in PRs when implementing routes — referencing add_concert.html, view_setlist.html, add_venue.html, and venues.html templates without including them breaks the application
Always implement proper error handling for database operations — missing try-catch blocks around database queries can cause application crashes and violates safe coding practices
Always add __pycache__/ to .gitignore and remove Python cache files from repository — committing __pycache__ directories violates code conventions and clutters version control
Remove unused imports to maintain clean code — importing EmailStr and validator from pydantic when validation is implemented as standalone methods violates code conventions
Integrate validation methods into models or remove them — defining validate_email() and validate_capacity() methods without connecting them to SQLAlchemy using @validates decorator creates dead code
Never manually manage database sessions by calling next(get_db()) and db.close() — use FastAPI dependency injection or proper session context management instead of unsafe manual session handling
Always validate input data before passing to model constructors — using Venue(**venue_data) without validation can cause SQLAlchemy errors or security issues from unexpected fields
Implement proper transactional database session management — ensure rollback capabilities if errors occur after db.add() but before db.commit() to prevent inconsistent session state
Always complete test files without truncation — incomplete files with truncated functions cause syntax errors and break test runs
Always add __pycache__/ and *.pyc files to .gitignore — Python cache files should never be committed to version control
Maintain backward compatibility when modifying API exports — removing existing schema exports breaks imports for dependent modules
Always verify all imported modules exist in the codebase before using them — importing from src.database that doesn't exist causes runtime ImportError
Add Python cache files (__pycache__, *.pyc) to .gitignore and remove from repository — cache files should never be committed to version control
Ensure database migrations match model definitions completely — missing columns like updated_at in migrations cause schema inconsistencies
Always add __pycache__/ to .gitignore and never commit Python cache files to version control — keep repository clean by excluding build artifacts
Never use CORS allow_origins=['*'] in production code — make CORS configuration environment-dependent or use specific origins for security
Always complete test files without truncation or syntax errors — incomplete files ending mid-statement like 'song2_id': song...' cause runtime failures
Never commit Python cache files to version control — add __pycache__/ to .gitignore and remove these files from the repository to keep it clean per project conventions
Always implement CSRF protection and XSS escaping when using HTMX forms — use Flask-WTF CSRF tokens on data-modifying endpoints (hx-post, hx-delete) and escape user content with {{ content|e }}
Extract large inline CSS to separate files for maintainability — avoid embedding 400+ lines of CSS directly in templates
Test public interface methods rather than private methods — avoid testing private methods like _validate_venue_data() directly and instead test the public methods that use them

Mock chained method calls require explicit return_value assignment at each level — use mock_db.query.return_value.filter.return_value.first.return_value instead of mock_db.query().filter().first.return_value
Always verify all imported modules and dependencies exist before referencing them — missing verification that VenueService, Venue model, and exception classes exist before using them in tests

Always include all referenced files in PRs and verify imports — file contained only backticks with database.py reference but database.py was missing, causing ModuleNotFoundError for 'src.database.create_tables'
Never commit Python cache files (__pycache__ directories) to version control — add __pycache__/ to .gitignore and remove these files to keep repository clean
Never remove essential dependencies that are still in use — pytest, pydantic, and alembic were removed from requirements but tests still import pytest and models need pydantic for schemas
Always verify all imported modules exist and are included in the PR — missing imports for database.py, models.py, and schemas.py modules will cause import errors and application startup failure
Always implement pagination on collection GET endpoints — missing limit/offset parameters on GET /concerts/ endpoint violates architecture decisions and causes performance issues with large datasets
Always provide complete files without truncation in PRs — truncated files at specific lines can cause syntax errors and make code review impossible
Quality gate 'coverage' failed — {"percent": 0.0, "threshold": 70, "status": "fail"}
